sentences.sentence: The Persians, who lived in present-day Iran, were one of the first civilizations to build tunnels that provided a reliable supply of water to human settlements in dry areas.
sentences.translation: 生活在今天伊朗地区的波斯人，是最早建造隧道的文明之一；这些隧道能为干旱地区的人类聚居地提供可靠的供水。

structure.text: were one of the first civilizations
that provided a reliable supply of water to human settlements in dry areas

difficult_points: who lived in present-day Iran 是非限制性定语从句，补充说明 Persians 的所在地。
one of 后接复数名词 civilizations，表示“最早的文明之一”。
to build tunnels 修饰 civilizations，说明这些文明的特点。
that provided... 修饰 tunnels，说明隧道的功能。

sentences.sentence: In the early first millennium BCE, they introduced the qanat method of tunnel construction, which consisted of placing posts over a hill in a straight line, to ensure that the tunnel kept to its route, and then digging vertical shafts down into the ground at regular intervals.
sentences.translation: 在公元前第一个千年早期，他们引入了坎儿井式隧道建造方法：先在山丘上沿直线放置标杆，以确保隧道保持既定路线，然后每隔固定距离向地下挖竖井。

structure.text: In the early first millennium BCE
they introduced the qanat method of tunnel construction
which consisted of placing posts over a hill in a straight line
to ensure that the tunnel kept to its route
that the tunnel kept to its route
and then digging vertical shafts down into the ground at regular intervals

difficult_points: which 引导非限制性定语从句，解释 qanat method 的具体做法。
consisted of 后接两个并列动名词短语：placing... 和 digging...。
to ensure... 表示放置标杆的目的。
at regular intervals 表示竖井之间有固定间隔。

sentences.sentence: Underground, workers removed the earth from between the ends of the shafts, creating a tunnel.
sentences.translation: 在地下，工人们从各个竖井末端之间挖走泥土，从而形成一条隧道。

structure.text: from between the ends of the shafts

difficult_points: Underground 放在句首，说明挖土发生的位置。
from between the ends of the shafts 表示在竖井底端之间的区域挖走泥土。
creating a tunnel 是现在分词结果状语，说明移除泥土后的结果。

sentences.sentence: The excavated soil was taken up to the surface using the shafts, which also provided ventilation during the work.
sentences.translation: 挖出的土通过竖井被运到地表，而这些竖井在施工期间也提供通风。

structure.text: was taken up to the surface
which also provided ventilation during the work

difficult_points: excavated 是过去分词作定语，修饰 soil。
was taken up 是被动语态，说明土被运到地表。
which 指代 shafts，补充说明竖井还有通风作用。
using the shafts 表示运土的方式。

sentences.sentence: Once the tunnel was completed, it allowed water to flow from the top of a hillside down towards a canal, which supplied water for human use.
sentences.translation: 隧道一旦建成，就能让水从山坡顶部向下流向一条水渠，而这条水渠为人类用水供水。

structure.text: water to flow from the top of a hillside down towards a canal
from the top of a hillside down towards a canal
which supplied water for human use

difficult_points: Once 引导时间状语从句，表示“隧道一完工”。
it 指代 the tunnel。
allow + 宾语 + to do 表示“使……能够做某事”。
which 指代 canal，不是 hillside。

sentences.sentence: Remarkably, some qanats built by the Persians 2,700 years ago are still in use today.
sentences.translation: 令人惊讶的是，波斯人在2700年前建造的一些坎儿井今天仍在使用。

structure.text: some qanats built by the Persians 2,700 years ago
built by the Persians 2,700 years ago

difficult_points: Remarkably 是句子副词，表达作者对事实的评价。
built by the Persians 2,700 years ago 是过去分词短语，修饰 some qanats。
are still in use 表示“仍在使用”，强调古老工程的持久性。

sentences.sentence: They later passed on their knowledge to the Romans, who also used the qanat method to construct water-supply tunnels for agriculture.
sentences.translation: 后来他们把自己的知识传给了罗马人，而罗马人也使用坎儿井方法来建造农业供水隧道。

structure.text: later passed on their knowledge to the Romans
who also used the qanat method to construct water-supply tunnels for agriculture
to construct water-supply tunnels for agriculture

difficult_points: They 指前文的 Persians。
pass on... to... 表示把知识传递给别人。
who 引导非限制性定语从句，补充说明 Romans 也使用该方法。
to construct... 表示使用方法的目的。

sentences.sentence: Roman qanat tunnels were constructed with vertical shafts dug at intervals of between 30 and 60 meters.
sentences.translation: 罗马的坎儿井隧道是用每隔30到60米挖出的竖井建造的。

structure.text: with vertical shafts dug at intervals of between 30 and 60 meters
dug at intervals of between 30 and 60 meters

difficult_points: were constructed 是被动语态，说明隧道被建造的方式。
with vertical shafts... 表示建造中所使用的结构。
dug at intervals... 是过去分词短语，修饰 shafts。
between 30 and 60 meters 表示竖井间隔范围。

sentences.sentence: The shafts were equipped with handholds and footholds to help those climbing in and out of them and were covered with a wooden or stone lid.
sentences.translation: 竖井配有手抓点和脚踏点，以帮助进出竖井的人攀爬，并且竖井上盖有木盖或石盖。

structure.text: were equipped with handholds and footholds
to help those climbing in and out of them
those climbing in and out of them
and were covered with a wooden or stone lid

difficult_points: were equipped with 和 were covered with 共用主语 The shafts。
to help... 表示安装抓点和踏点的目的。
those climbing in and out of them 指进出竖井的人。
them 指 the shafts。

sentences.sentence: To ensure that the shafts were vertical, Romans hung a plumb line from a rod placed across the top of each shaft and made sure that the weight at the end of it hung in the center of the shaft.
sentences.translation: 为了确保竖井垂直，罗马人从横放在每个竖井顶部的杆子上悬下一条铅垂线，并确保铅垂线末端的重物悬在竖井中央。

structure.text: To ensure that the shafts were vertical
Romans hung a plumb line from a rod placed across the top of each shaft
placed across the top of each shaft
and made sure that the weight at the end of it hung in the center of the shaft
that the weight at the end of it hung in the center of the shaft

difficult_points: To ensure... 是目的状语，说明使用铅垂线的目的。
placed across... 是过去分词短语，修饰 a rod。
made sure 后接 that 从句，说明需要确认的状态。
it 指 a plumb line，the weight at the end of it 指铅垂线末端的重物。

sentences.sentence: Plumb lines were also used to measure the depth of the shaft and to determine the slope of the tunnel.
sentences.translation: 铅垂线还被用来测量竖井深度，并确定隧道的坡度。

structure.text: to measure the depth of the shaft
to determine the slope of the tunnel

difficult_points: were used 是被动语态，说明铅垂线的用途。
also 表明铅垂线除保证竖直外还有其他用途。
to measure 和 to determine 是并列目的。

sentences.sentence: The 5.6-kilometer-long Claudius tunnel, built in 41 CE to drain the Fucine Lake in central Italy, had shafts that were up to 122 meters deep, took 11 years to build and involved approximately 30,000 workers.
sentences.translation: 长5.6公里的克劳狄乌斯隧道建于公元41年，用于排干意大利中部的富奇诺湖；它的竖井深达122米，耗时11年建成，并动用了约3万名工人。

structure.text: built in 41 CE to drain the Fucine Lake in central Italy
to drain the Fucine Lake in central Italy
had shafts that were up to 122 meters deep
that were up to 122 meters deep
took 11 years to build and involved approximately 30,000 workers

difficult_points: built in 41 CE... 是过去分词短语，补充说明 Claudius tunnel。
to drain... 表示建造该隧道的目的。
had、took、involved 是三个并列谓语，共用同一主语。
that were up to 122 meters deep 修饰 shafts。

sentences.sentence: By the 6th century BCE, a second method of tunnel construction appeared called the counter-excavation method, in which the tunnel was constructed from both ends.
sentences.translation: 到公元前6世纪，第二种隧道建造方法出现了，称为对向开挖法；在这种方法中，隧道从两端同时建造。

structure.text: a second method of tunnel construction appeared
in which the tunnel was constructed from both ends
the tunnel was constructed from both ends

difficult_points: By the 6th century BCE 表示截至公元前6世纪这一时间点。
called... 是过去分词短语，修饰 a second method。
in which 引导定语从句，which 指 counter-excavation method。
was constructed from both ends 是被动语态，说明该方法的核心做法。

sentences.sentence: It was used to cut through high mountains when the qanat method was not a practical alternative.
sentences.translation: 当坎儿井方法不是一种可行替代方案时，这种方法被用来穿过高山。

structure.text: when the qanat method was not a practical alternative
the qanat method was not a practical alternative

difficult_points: It 指前一句的 counter-excavation method。
was used to do 表示“被用来做某事”。
when 引导条件兼时间背景，说明何时采用这种方法。
practical alternative 指实际可行的替代方案。

sentences.sentence: This method required greater planning and advanced knowledge of surveying, mathematics and geometry as both ends of a tunnel had to meet correctly at the center of the mountain.
sentences.translation: 这种方法需要更多规划，以及测量、数学和几何方面的高级知识，因为隧道两端必须在山体中心准确会合。

structure.text: greater planning and advanced knowledge of surveying, mathematics and geometry
as both ends of a tunnel had to meet correctly at the center of the mountain
both ends of a tunnel had to meet correctly at the center of the mountain

difficult_points: required 后接并列宾语 greater planning 和 advanced knowledge。
of surveying, mathematics and geometry 修饰 knowledge，说明知识领域。
as 引导原因状语从句，解释为什么需要更高规划和知识。
had to meet correctly 强调两端会合必须准确。

sentences.sentence: Adjustments to the direction of the tunnel also had to be made whenever builders encountered geological problems or when it deviated from its set path.
sentences.translation: 每当建造者遇到地质问题，或者隧道偏离既定路径时，也必须对隧道方向进行调整。

structure.text: Adjustments to the direction of the tunnel
when it deviated from its set path

difficult_points: Adjustments 是主语，to the direction... 说明调整的对象。
had to be made 是被动语态，表示必须做出调整。
whenever 和 when 引导两个并列情况。
it 指 tunnel，its set path 指隧道预定路线。

sentences.sentence: They constantly checked the tunnel's advancing direction, for example, by looking back at the light that penetrated through the tunnel mouth, and made corrections whenever necessary.
sentences.translation: 他们不断检查隧道推进的方向，例如回头观察从隧道口透进来的光，并在必要时进行修正。

structure.text: They constantly checked the tunnel's advancing direction
by looking back at the light
that penetrated through the tunnel mouth

difficult_points: They 指 builders。
for example 插入说明检查方向的一种方法。
that 引导定语从句，修饰 the light。
whenever necessary 是省略结构，完整含义是 whenever it was necessary。

sentences.sentence: Large deviations could happen, and they could result in one end of the tunnel not being usable.
sentences.translation: 大的偏差可能会发生，并且可能导致隧道的一端无法使用。

structure.text: they could result in one end of the tunnel not being usable
one end of the tunnel not being usable

difficult_points: could 表示可能性，而不是过去能力。
they 指 Large deviations。
result in 后接名词性结构，表示导致某种结果。
not being usable 是动名词被动结构中的否定形式。

sentences.sentence: An inscription written on the side of a 428-meter tunnel, built by the Romans as part of the Saldae aqueduct system in modern-day Algeria, describes how the two teams of builders missed each other in the mountain and how the later construction of a lateral link between both corridors corrected the initial error.
sentences.translation: 一段刻在一条428米长隧道侧壁上的铭文描述了两队建造者如何在山中错过彼此，以及后来如何通过在两条通道之间建造一条横向连接来纠正最初的错误；这条隧道由罗马人建造，是现代阿尔及利亚境内 Saldae 渡槽系统的一部分。

structure.text: An inscription written on the side of a 428-meter tunnel
written on the side of a 428-meter tunnel
built by the Romans as part of the Saldae aqueduct system in modern-day Algeria
how the two teams of builders missed each other in the mountain
and how the later construction of a lateral link between both corridors corrected the initial error

difficult_points: 主语核心是 An inscription，中间两个分词短语补充说明铭文和隧道。
built by the Romans... 修饰 tunnel，不是 inscription。
describes 后接两个 how 引导的并列宾语从句。
corrected the initial error 指后来建横向连接修正了最初偏差。

sentences.sentence: The Romans dug tunnels for their roads using the counter-excavation method, whenever they encountered obstacles such as hills or mountains that were too high for roads to pass over.
sentences.translation: 每当罗马人在修路时遇到山丘或高到道路无法翻越的山脉等障碍物，他们就使用对向开挖法为道路挖掘隧道。

structure.text: The Romans dug tunnels for their roads
whenever they encountered obstacles such as hills or mountains
that were too high for roads to pass over

difficult_points: using... 是现在分词短语，说明挖隧道所用方法。
whenever 引导时间状语从句，表示每当遇到障碍时。
such as hills or mountains 举例说明 obstacles。
that were too high... 修饰 hills or mountains。

expressions.text: too high for roads to pass over

sentences.sentence: An example is the 37-meter-long, 6-meter-high Furlo Pass Tunnel built in Italy in 69–79 CE.
sentences.translation: 一个例子是公元69至79年在意大利建造的 Furlo Pass 隧道，它长37米、高6米。
difficult_points: An example 指道路隧道的一个实例。
37-meter-long 和 6-meter-high 是复合形容词，修饰 Furlo Pass Tunnel。
built in Italy in 69–79 CE 是过去分词短语，说明建造地点和时间。

sentences.sentence: Remarkably, a modern road still uses this tunnel today.
sentences.translation: 令人惊讶的是，如今一条现代道路仍在使用这条隧道。
difficult_points: Remarkably 表示作者认为这个事实很不寻常。
still uses 强调古代隧道一直延续到现代使用。
this tunnel 指前一句的 Furlo Pass Tunnel。

expressions.meaning_zh: 今天仍在使用这条隧道

sentences.sentence: Tunnels were also built for mineral extraction.
sentences.translation: 隧道也被建造用于矿物开采。
difficult_points: were built 是被动语态，强调隧道的用途而非建造者。
also 表示除了道路和供水以外还有采矿用途。
for mineral extraction 表示建造目的。

sentences.sentence: Miners would locate a mineral vein and then pursue it with shafts and tunnels underground.
sentences.translation: 矿工会先找到一条矿脉，然后在地下用竖井和隧道追踪它。

structure.text: pursue it with shafts and tunnels underground

difficult_points: would 表示过去常见做法，不是虚拟语气。
locate 和 pursue 是顺序动作，先找矿脉再沿着它开挖。
it 指 a mineral vein。
with shafts and tunnels 表示追踪矿脉所用手段。

expressions.text: pursue it with shafts and tunnels

sentences.sentence: Traces of such tunnels used to mine gold can still be found at the Dolaucothi mines in Wales.
sentences.translation: 这种用于采金的隧道遗迹今天仍可在威尔士的 Dolaucothi 矿区找到。

structure.text: Traces of such tunnels used to mine gold
at the Dolaucothi mines in Wales

difficult_points: Traces 是主语核心，of such tunnels 限定是什么遗迹。
used to mine gold 是过去分词短语，修饰 tunnels，表示隧道用途。
can still be found 是情态动词加被动语态，强调遗迹仍存在。
at the Dolaucothi mines in Wales 给出发现地点。

sentences.sentence: When the sole purpose of a tunnel was mineral extraction, construction required less planning, as the tunnel route was determined by the mineral vein.
sentences.translation: 当隧道的唯一目的是矿物开采时，建造所需规划较少，因为隧道路线由矿脉决定。

structure.text: When the sole purpose of a tunnel was mineral extraction
the sole purpose of a tunnel
as the tunnel route was determined by the mineral vein
was determined by the mineral vein

difficult_points: When 引导时间或条件状语从句，说明规划较少的情况。
sole purpose 表示唯一目的，排除了供水或道路等其他用途。
as 引导原因状语从句。
was determined by 是被动语态，说明路线由矿脉决定。

sentences.sentence: Roman tunnel projects were carefully planned and carried out.
sentences.translation: 罗马隧道项目经过精心规划和实施。

structure.text: were carefully planned and carried out

difficult_points: were planned and carried out 是被动语态，两个动作并列。
carefully 修饰 planned and carried out，强调规划和执行都很谨慎。
句子概括引出本段对工期和施工因素的说明。

expressions.text: be carefully planned and carried out

sentences.sentence: The length of time it took to construct a tunnel depended on the method being used and the type of rock being excavated.
sentences.translation: 建造一条隧道所需的时间长短取决于所使用的方法和正在开挖的岩石类型。

structure.text: The length of time it took to construct a tunnel
it took to construct a tunnel
the method being used and the type of rock being excavated

difficult_points: 主语较长，核心是 The length of time。
it took to construct a tunnel 修饰 time，表示“建造隧道所花的时间”。
depended on 后接两个并列因素：method 和 type of rock。
being used / being excavated 都是现在分词被动形式，说明正在使用或开挖的对象。

sentences.sentence: The qanat construction method was usually faster than the counter-excavation method as it was more straightforward.
sentences.translation: 坎儿井建造法通常比对向开挖法更快，因为它更直接简单。

structure.text: was usually faster than the counter-excavation method

difficult_points: faster than 构成比较结构，对比两种隧道建造方法。
as 引导原因状语从句，解释坎儿井方法更快的原因。
it 指 the qanat construction method。
straightforward 表示步骤更直接、复杂度更低。

sentences.sentence: This was because the mountain could be excavated not only from the tunnel mouths but also from shafts.
sentences.translation: 这是因为山体不仅可以从隧道口开挖，也可以从竖井开挖。

structure.text: because the mountain could be excavated not only from the tunnel mouths but also from shafts
not only from the tunnel mouths but also from shafts

difficult_points: This 指上一句“坎儿井方法通常更快”这一结论。
because 引导表语从句，解释原因。
could be excavated 是情态动词加被动语态。
not only... but also... 强调可以从两个位置同时开挖。

sentences.sentence: The type of rock could also influence construction times.
sentences.translation: 岩石类型也会影响建造时间。
difficult_points: could 表示可能性，说明岩石类型是影响工期的因素。
also 表明除施工方法外，岩石类型同样重要。
construction times 指建造所需时间，而不是具体施工时刻。

sentences.sentence: When the rock was hard, the Romans employed a technique called fire quenching which consisted of heating the rock with fire, and then suddenly cooling it with cold water so that it would crack.
sentences.translation: 当岩石很坚硬时，罗马人采用一种叫作火淬的技术，这种技术包括用火加热岩石，然后突然用冷水冷却岩石，使其开裂。

structure.text: which consisted of heating the rock with fire
and then suddenly cooling it with cold water

difficult_points: When 引导条件或时间状语从句，说明使用 fire quenching 的情况。
called fire quenching 是过去分词短语，修饰 a technique。
which 指 fire quenching 这种技术，解释其步骤。
so that 引导目的或结果从句，说明冷热处理使岩石开裂。

sentences.sentence: Progress through hard rock could be very slow, and it was not uncommon for tunnels to take years, if not decades, to be built.
sentences.translation: 在坚硬岩石中推进可能非常缓慢，隧道花费数年，甚至数十年建成并不罕见。

structure.text: Progress through hard rock could be very slow
it was not uncommon for tunnels to take years

difficult_points: could be very slow 表示推进速度可能很慢。
it was not uncommon for... 是形式主语结构，真正内容是 for tunnels to take years...。
if not decades 表示“甚至可能是几十年”，加强 years 的程度。
to be built 是被动不定式，表示隧道被建成。

sentences.sentence: Construction marks left on a Roman tunnel in Bologna show that the rate of advance through solid rock was 30 centimeters per day.
sentences.translation: 博洛尼亚一条罗马隧道上留下的施工痕迹显示，在坚硬岩石中推进的速度为每天30厘米。

structure.text: Construction marks left on a Roman tunnel in Bologna
left on a Roman tunnel in Bologna
that the rate of advance through solid rock was 30 centimeters per day

difficult_points: 主语核心是 Construction marks，left... 是过去分词短语修饰它。
show 后接 that 宾语从句，说明这些痕迹显示的内容。
the rate of advance 指施工推进速度。
per day 表示“每天”。

sentences.sentence: In contrast, the rate of advance of the Claudius tunnel can be calculated at 1.4 meters per day.
sentences.translation: 相比之下，克劳狄乌斯隧道的推进速度可以计算为每天1.4米。

structure.text: the rate of advance of the Claudius tunnel

difficult_points: In contrast 表明本句与前一句30厘米每天形成对比。
can be calculated 是情态动词加被动语态。
at 1.4 meters per day 表示计算得到的推进速度。
of the Claudius tunnel 限定是哪条隧道的速度。

sentences.sentence: Most tunnels had inscriptions showing the names of patrons who ordered construction and sometimes the name of the architect.
sentences.translation: 大多数隧道都有铭文，显示下令建造的资助者姓名，有时也显示建筑师的姓名。

structure.text: and sometimes the name of the architect

difficult_points: showing... 是现在分词短语，修饰 inscriptions。
who 引导定语从句，修饰 patrons。
patrons 指出资或委托建造的人。
and sometimes... 与 the names of patrons 并列，表示铭文也可能显示建筑师姓名。

sentences.sentence: For example, the 1.4-kilometer Çevlik tunnel in Turkey, built to divert the floodwater threatening the harbor of the ancient city of Seleuceia Pieria, had inscriptions on the entrance, still visible today, that also indicate that the tunnel was started in 69 CE and was completed in 81 CE.
sentences.translation: 例如，土耳其1.4公里长的 Çevlik 隧道是为了转移威胁古城 Seleuceia Pieria 港口的洪水而建；它入口处的铭文今天仍可见，并且还表明该隧道始建于公元69年，完成于公元81年。

structure.text: built to divert the floodwater threatening the harbor of the ancient city of Seleuceia Pieria
to divert the floodwater threatening the harbor of the ancient city of Seleuceia Pieria
that also indicate that the tunnel was started in 69 CE and was completed in 81 CE

difficult_points: built to divert... 是过去分词短语，修饰 Çevlik tunnel，并说明建造目的。
threatening the harbor... 是现在分词短语，修饰 floodwater。
still visible today 插入说明 inscriptions 直到今天仍可见。
that also indicate... 修饰 inscriptions，后面的 that 从句是 indicate 的宾语。

expressions.text: was started in 69 CE and was completed in 81 CE
expressions.meaning_zh: 始建于公元69年并于公元81年完成
